title: Valid credentials return HTTP 200 and session token
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account exists with username 'alice' and a valid bcrypt-hashed password for 'Secret1!'
steps: POST /api/auth/login with body {"username":"alice","password":"Secret1!"}
expected_result: HTTP 200 response containing a signed session token; token is non-empty and structurally valid.

title: Wrong password returns HTTP 401 and no token
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account exists with username 'alice'
steps: POST /api/auth/login with body {"username":"alice","password":"WrongPass!"}
expected_result: HTTP 401 response; response body contains no session token.

title: Unknown username returns HTTP 401 and no token
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
No user account exists with username 'ghost'
steps: POST /api/auth/login with body {"username":"ghost","password":"Secret1!"}
expected_result: HTTP 401 response; response body contains no session token and leaks no information about whether the username exists.

title: Empty password field returns HTTP 400
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
steps: POST /api/auth/login with body {"username":"alice","password":""}
expected_result: HTTP 400 response indicating that required fields are missing; no session token is issued.

title: Successful login issues a signed session token
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account exists with username 'bob' and known password 'Pass2@secure'
steps: POST /api/auth/login with body {"username":"bob","password":"Pass2@secure"}
Extract the session token from the response body
Verify the token signature using the server's public signing key
expected_result: Token signature verification succeeds; token is not empty and is parseable as a signed JWT or equivalent structure.

title: Session token contains the correct role claim for a student
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Student account exists with username 'student1' and known password
steps: POST /api/auth/login with body {"username":"student1","password":"StudentPass1!"}
Decode the returned session token without verifying signature
Inspect the role claim in the token payload
expected_result: Token payload contains a role claim equal to 'student'.

title: Session token contains the correct role claim for an admin
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Admin account exists with username 'admin1' and known password
steps: POST /api/auth/login with body {"username":"admin1","password":"AdminPass1!"}
Decode the returned session token
Inspect the role claim in the token payload
expected_result: Token payload contains a role claim equal to 'admin'.

title: Token role claim unit test verifies embed at issuance
steps: Call the token-issuance function with a user struct where Role is set to 'student'
Decode the returned token string
Assert that the decoded claims contain the role field matching the input
expected_result: The decoded token's role field equals 'student', confirming the claim is embedded at issuance time.

title: Student token is rejected when accessing an admin-only endpoint
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
A valid session token exists with role claim 'student'
steps: Send GET /api/admin/users with Authorization header containing the student session token
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; the request is not processed and no admin data is returned.

title: Admin token is accepted on an admin-only endpoint
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
A valid session token exists with role claim 'admin'
steps: Send GET /api/admin/users with Authorization header containing the admin session token
expected_result: HTTP 200 response; admin resource data is returned in the response body.

title: Request without any token is rejected on a protected endpoint
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
steps: Send GET /api/student/courses with no Authorization header
expected_result: HTTP 401 response; no course data is returned.

title: Tampered token is rejected on a protected endpoint
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
A valid session token has been obtained for 'student1'
steps: Modify one character in the signature section of the token
Send GET /api/student/courses with Authorization header containing the tampered token
expected_result: HTTP 401 response; the server rejects the request due to invalid token signature.

title: Expired session token is rejected on a protected endpoint
preconditions: Inactivity expiry is configured to 1 second for the test
steps: Issue a session token using the token-issuance function with a clock fixed 2 seconds in the past
Call the token-validation function with the current time and the token
expected_result: Token validation returns an expiry error; the token is not accepted.

title: Active token within inactivity window is accepted
preconditions: Inactivity expiry is configured to 30 minutes for the test
steps: Issue a session token at time T
Call the token-validation function at time T+5 minutes
expected_result: Token validation succeeds; the token is accepted as valid.

title: Token exactly at the expiry boundary is rejected
preconditions: Inactivity expiry is configured to 30 minutes for the test
steps: Issue a session token at time T
Call the token-validation function at exactly T+30 minutes
expected_result: Token validation returns an expiry error; the token at the boundary is not accepted.

title: Account is locked after five consecutive failed login attempts
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account exists with username 'carol' and known password
Failed attempt counter for 'carol' is at zero
steps: POST /api/auth/login five times with body {"username":"carol","password":"WrongPass!"}
POST /api/auth/login a sixth time with the correct password for 'carol'
expected_result: The sixth request returns HTTP 423 or HTTP 401 indicating the account is locked; login is not granted even with the correct password.

title: Account is not locked after four failed login attempts
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account exists with username 'dave' and known password
Failed attempt counter for 'dave' is at zero
steps: POST /api/auth/login four times with body {"username":"dave","password":"WrongPass!"}
POST /api/auth/login with the correct password for 'dave'
expected_result: HTTP 200 response with a valid session token; the account is accessible after four failures.

title: Locked account is automatically unlocked after configurable lockout duration
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Lockout duration is configured to 2 seconds for the test
User account 'eve' has been locked by five failed attempts
steps: Wait for the lockout duration to elapse
POST /api/auth/login with the correct password for 'eve'
expected_result: HTTP 200 response with a valid session token; the lockout has expired and login succeeds.

title: Successful login resets failed attempt counter to zero
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
User account 'frank' has accumulated three failed login attempts
steps: POST /api/auth/login with the correct password for 'frank'
POST /api/auth/login four times with wrong passwords for 'frank'
expected_result: After the successful login the counter resets; four subsequent failures do not lock the account and a fifth failure triggers lockout only on the fifth attempt after the reset.

title: Token with tampered role claim but intact signature sections is rejected with HTTP 401
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
A valid session token has been obtained for student account 'student1' with role claim 'student'
steps: Decode the header and payload sections of the student session token using base64url decoding
Modify the role field in the decoded payload from 'student' to 'admin'
Re-encode the modified payload using base64url encoding, keeping the original header and signature sections unchanged
Reassemble the token as modified-header.modified-payload.original-signature
Send GET /api/admin/users with Authorization header containing the reassembled token
expected_result: HTTP 401 response; the server rejects the token because the signature no longer matches the modified payload, before any role check is performed.

title: Expired inactivity token is rejected at the handler level with HTTP 401
preconditions: PostgreSQL instance is running via Docker Compose
Inactivity timeout is configured to 2 seconds via the INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS environment variable
Student account 'student1' exists with a known password
steps: POST /api/auth/login with student1's credentials and record the returned session token
Wait 3 seconds for the inactivity window to elapse
Send GET /api/student/courses with Authorization header containing the recorded session token
expected_result: HTTP 401 response; the inactivity expiry middleware has invalidated the token and the request is rejected without returning any course data.
